text: Continuous Integration, often called CI, is a software development practice where team members regularly merge their code changes into a shared main branch. Each time new code is added, a CI pipeline automatically triggers a series of automated builds. These builds compile the code and then run tests to check if everything works correctly. This process is crucial for catching issues early, before they grow into bigger problems. Because developers integrate their work frequently, sometimes several times a day, it becomes easier to identify errors quickly. As a result, CI helps teams maintain high quality in their projects and reduces the time spent fixing bugs later.
